text: In the operating room, a varus needle is inserted into the umbilicus to establish pneumoperitoneum, followed by the placement of a 10mm trocar below the xiphoid to the left of midline. Under direct visualization, five additional trocars are placed: 5mm and 12mm trocars in the left upper quadrant, a 5mm port in the subxiphoid space, and 5mm and 12mm trocars in the right upper quadrant. Upon exploration, a retrosternal diaphragmatic defect is observed with herniation of the entire small bowel, omentum, and transverse colon. The hernia is incarcerated and cannot be reduced initially. Dissection begins with blunt traction on the right of the defect, dividing adhesions and removing the falciform ligament with traction and ligature. The anterior and posterior portions of the hernia sac are mobilized, leading to the complete reduction of the hernia contents.

The surgeon measures the diaphragmatic defect at approximately ten by six centimeters. A Jackson Pratt drain is placed in the right lower thoracic cavity and exits through the five millimeter subxiphoid port. The surrounding tissue is cleaned of debris, and a ten centimeter by fifteen centimeter polyester parietex composite mesh is sutured circumferentially with number one proline to repair the defect.
